title: cynoglossum furcatum var. lanceolatum
intro: Cynoglossum furcatum var. lanceolatum, also known as the forked hound's tongue or forked tongue, is a plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to China and other parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Cynoglossum furcatum var. lanceolatum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
